title: dryopteris alfaroi
intro: Dryopteris alfaroi, also known as Alfaró's Wood Fern, is a species of fern native to the Iberian Peninsula. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Dryopteris alfaroi is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential applications.
